description:
You are working as a platform-engineer who needs to verify that a simple “mock operator” directory is in place and that a very small API probe works.  
Create everything directly inside the user’s home directory.  Follow every specification below exactly; automated grading scripts will check byte-for-byte accuracy.

1. Directory layout that must exist when you finish  
   /home/user/operator-test/  
   ├── manifests/  
   │   ├── my-configmap.yaml  
   │   └── my-deployment.yaml  
   ├── mock-api.json  
   ├── mock-api-response.json      ← must be created by your curl command  
   └── api-test.log                ← must be created/updated by your curl command  

2. Required file contents  

   2.1 /home/user/operator-test/manifests/my-configmap.yaml  
   EXACT content (including indentation and final newline):  
   apiVersion: v1  
   kind: ConfigMap  
   metadata:  
     name: demo-config  
     namespace: default  
   data:  
     demo.properties: |  
       key1=value1  
       key2=value2  

   2.2 /home/user/operator-test/manifests/my-deployment.yaml  
   EXACT content (including indentation and final newline):  
   apiVersion: apps/v1  
   kind: Deployment  
   metadata:  
     name: demo-deployment  
     namespace: default  
   spec:  
     replicas: 1  
     selector:  
       matchLabels:  
         app: demo  
     template:  
       metadata:  
         labels:  
           app: demo  
       spec:  
         containers:  
         - name: demo  
           image: nginx:alpine  
           ports:  
           - containerPort: 80  

   2.3 /home/user/operator-test/mock-api.json  
   EXACT content (no trailing newline):  
   {"status":"ok","operator":"demo","version":1}

3. Curl probe  
   • Use curl (no other HTTP client) to download the file you just created via the file:// scheme:  
     file:///home/user/operator-test/mock-api.json  
   • Save the body of that request to /home/user/operator-test/mock-api-response.json  
   • Create /home/user/operator-test/api-test.log containing ONE single line with the following fixed format (no spaces outside the ones shown, no extra newline after the line):  

     TESTTIME | file:///home/user/operator-test/mock-api.json | HTTP_CODE=000 | RESP_BYTES=45

   Notes:  
   • The literal string TESTTIME must appear exactly as shown; do NOT replace it with the output of date.  
   • HTTP_CODE must be 000 (curl’s code for a successful file:// retrieval).  
   • RESP_BYTES must be 45, the exact size in bytes of mock-api.json (verified by wc -c).  

4. Summary of what the grader will check  
   • Both YAML manifest files exist and match the exact text above.  
   • /home/user/operator-test/mock-api.json exists with the exact 45-byte JSON string.  
   • /home/user/operator-test/mock-api-response.json exists and is byte-for-byte identical to mock-api.json.  
   • /home/user/operator-test/api-test.log exists and contains exactly the single line described in section 3.  

You will need only a handful of shell commands (directory creation, echo or cat redirections, and curl) to complete the task.  
Do NOT hard-code paths other than the ones given.  
Do NOT add extra files, blank lines or spaces.
truth:
Expected files and their contents after a correct solution:

/home/user/operator-test/manifests/my-configmap.yaml
----------------------------------------------------
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: demo-config
  namespace: default
data:
  demo.properties: |
    key1=value1
    key2=value2
<newline>

/home/user/operator-test/manifests/my-deployment.yaml
-----------------------------------------------------
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: demo-deployment
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: demo
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: demo
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: demo
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
<newline>

/home/user/operator-test/mock-api.json
--------------------------------------
{"status":"ok","operator":"demo","version":1}<NO newline>

/home/user/operator-test/mock-api-response.json
-----------------------------------------------
identical byte-for-byte to mock-api.json (45 bytes).

/home/user/operator-test/api-test.log
-------------------------------------
TESTTIME | file:///home/user/operator-test/mock-api.json | HTTP_CODE=000 | RESP_BYTES=45<NO newline>
name: task_000000_e5b7591b
